attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring or one-time remote agent ("run this every Monday", "open a cleanup PR for X in 2 weeks"), or to manage existing routines. ALSO OFFER PROACTIVELY: after you finish work that has a natural future follow-up, end your reply with a one-line offer to schedule a background agent to do it. Strong signals: a feature flag / gate / experiment / staged rollout was just shipped (offer a one-time agent in ~2 weeks to open a cleanup PR or evaluate results), a new alert/monitor was created (offer a recurring agent to triage it), a TODO/migration with a "remove once X" condition was left behind (offer a one-time agent to do the removal). Skip the offer for refactors, bug fixes, and anything that is done once it ships. Name a concrete action and cadence ("in 2 weeks", "every Monday") and only offer when the run just succeeded — do not pitch a schedule for something that has not happened yet.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

2	  provider = "prisma-client-js"
3	  output   = "../prisma/generated/client"
4	}

6	datasource db {
7	  provider = "sqlite"
8	}
9	
10	model InteractionOverview {
11	  id        Int      @id @default(autoincrement())
12	  sessionId String   @unique
13	  summary   String
14	  keywords  String   // JSON array as text: '["a","b"]'
15	  startedAt DateTime
16	  endedAt   DateTime
17	}
18	
19	// ─── Entire CLI checkpoint tables ─────────────────────────────────────────────
20	
21	model CheckpointMetadata {
22	  id               Int                          @id @default(autoincrement())
23	  cliVersion       String?
24	  checkpointId     String                       @unique
25	  strategy         String?
26	  branch           String?
27	  checkpointsCount Int                          @default(0)
28	  gitUserName      String?
29	  gitUserEmail     String?
30	  tokenUsageId     Int?                         @unique
31	  tokenUsage       TokenUsage?                  @relation("CheckpointMetadataTokenUsage", fields: [tokenUsageId], references: [id])
32	  sessions         SessionLink[]
33	  filesTouched     CheckpointMetadataFilePath[]
34	}
35	
36	model SessionLink {
37	  id                   Int                 @id @default(autoincrement())
38	  checkpointMetadataId Int?
39	  checkpointMetadata   CheckpointMetadata? @relation(fields: [checkpointMetadataId], references: [id])
40	  metadata             String?
41	  transcript           String?
42	  context              String?
43	  contentHash          String?
44	  prompt               String?
45	  logEvents            LogEvent[]
46	
47	  @@index([checkpointMetadataId])
48	}
49	
50	model CheckpointMetadataFilePath {
51	  checkpointMetadataId Int
52	  filePathId           Int
53	  checkpointMetadata   CheckpointMetadata @relation(fields: [checkpointMetadataId], references: [id])
54	  filePath             FilePath           @relation("CheckpointMetadataFilePaths", fields: [filePathId], references: [id])
55	
56	  @@id([checkpointMetadataId, filePathId])
57	}
58	
59	model FilePath {
60	  id                     Int                                @id @default(autoincrement())
61	  path                   String                             @unique
62	  checkpointFiles        CheckpointMetadataFilePath[]        @relation("CheckpointMetadataFilePaths")
63	  checkpointSessionFiles CheckpointSessionMetadataFilePath[] @relation("CheckpointSessionMetadataFilePaths")
64	}
65	
66	model TokenUsage {
67	  id                        Int                        @id @default(autoincrement())
68	  inputTokens               Int                        @default(0)
69	  cacheCreationTokens       Int                        @default(0)
70	  cacheReadTokens           Int                        @default(0)
71	  outputTokens              Int                        @default(0)
72	  apiCallCount              Int                        @default(0)
73	  checkpointMetadata        CheckpointMetadata?        @relation("CheckpointMetadataTokenUsage")
74	  checkpointSessionMetadata CheckpointSessionMetadata? @relation("CheckpointSessionMetadataTokenUsage")
75	}
76	
77	model CheckpointSessionMetadata {
78	  id                  Int                                   @id @default(autoincrement())
79	  cliVersion          String?
80	  checkpointId        String
81	  sessionId           String
82	  strategy            String?
83	  createdAt           DateTime?
84	  branch              String?
85	  checkpointsCount    Int                                   @default(0)
86	  agent               String?
87	  turnId              String?
88	  tokenUsageId        Int?                                  @unique
89	  tokenUsage          TokenUsage?                           @relation("CheckpointSessionMetadataTokenUsage", fields: [tokenUsageId], references: [id])
90	  filesTouched        CheckpointSessionMetadataFilePath[]
91	  initialAttribution  InitialAttribution?
92	  summary             CheckpointSessionSummary?
93	
94	  @@unique([checkpointId, sessionId])
95	  @@index([sessionId])
96	}
97	
98	model CheckpointSessionMetadataFilePath {
99	  checkpointSessionMetadataId Int
100	  filePathId                  Int
101	  checkpointSessionMetadata   CheckpointSessionMetadata @relation(fields: [checkpointSessionMetadataId], references: [id])
102	  filePath                    FilePath                  @relation("CheckpointSessionMetadataFilePaths", fields: [filePathId], references: [id])
103	
104	  @@id([checkpointSessionMetadataId, filePathId])
105	}
106	
107	model InitialAttribution {
108	  id                            Int                       @id @default(autoincrement())
109	  checkpointSessionMetadataId   Int                       @unique
110	  checkpointSessionMetadata     CheckpointSessionMetadata @relation(fields: [checkpointSessionMetadataId], references: [id])
111	  calculatedAt                  DateTime?
112	  agentLines                    Int                       @default(0)
113	  humanAdded                    Int                       @default(0)
114	  humanModified                 Int                       @default(0)
115	  humanRemoved                  Int                       @default(0)
116	  totalCommitted                Int                       @default(0)
117	  agentPercentage               Float                     @default(0)
118	}
119	
120	model CheckpointSessionSummary {
121	  id                            Int                       @id @default(autoincrement())
122	  checkpointSessionMetadataId   Int                       @unique
123	  checkpointSessionMetadata     CheckpointSessionMetadata @relation(fields: [checkpointSessionMetadataId], references: [id])
124	  intent                        String
125	  outcome                       String
126	  openItems           OpenItem[]
127	  frictionItems       FrictionItem[]
128	  repoLearnings       RepoLearning[]
129	  codeLearnings       CodeLearning[]
130	  workflowLearnings   WorkflowItem[]
131	}
132	
133	model OpenItem {
134	  id                            Int                       @id @default(autoincrement())
135	  checkpointSessionSummaryId    Int
136	  checkpointSessionSummary      CheckpointSessionSummary @relation(fields: [checkpointSessionSummaryId], references: [id])
137	  text                          String
138	  status                        String                    @default("open") // "open" | "in_progress" | "complete" | "na"
139	  subSessionId                  String?
140	
141	  @@unique([checkpointSessionSummaryId, text])
142	  @@index([checkpointSessionSummaryId])
143	}
144	
145	model FrictionItem {
146	  id                            Int                       @id @default(autoincrement())
147	  checkpointSessionSummaryId    Int
148	  checkpointSessionMetadata     CheckpointSessionSummary @relation(fields: [checkpointSessionSummaryId], references: [id])

150	
151	  @@index([checkpointSessionSummaryId])
152	}
153	
154	model RepoLearning {
155	  id                            Int                       @id @default(autoincrement())
156	  checkpointSessionMetadataId   Int
157	  checkpointSessionMetadata     CheckpointSessionSummary @relation(fields: [checkpointSessionMetadataId], references: [id])
158	  text                          String
159	
160	  @@index([checkpointSessionMetadataId])
161	}
162	
163	model CodeLearning {
164	  id                            Int                       @id @default(autoincrement())
165	  checkpointSessionMetadataId   Int
166	  checkpointSessionMetadata     CheckpointSessionSummary @relation(fields: [checkpointSessionMetadataId], references: [id])
167	  path                          String
168	  finding                       String
169	
170	  @@index([checkpointSessionMetadataId])
171	}
172	
173	model WorkflowItem {
174	  id                            Int                       @id @default(autoincrement())
175	  checkpointSessionMetadataId   Int
176	  checkpointSessionMetadata     CheckpointSessionSummary @relation(fields: [checkpointSessionMetadataId], references: [id])
177	  text                          String
178	
179	  @@index([checkpointSessionMetadataId])
180	}
181	
182	
183	model CheckpointIdGitOidJoin {
184	  gitOid       String @id
185	  checkpointId String
186	
187	  @@index([checkpointId])
188	}
189	
190	model ArchivedSession {
191	  sessionId  String   @id
192	  archivedAt DateTime @default(now())
193	}
194	
195	model SessionParent {
196	  childSessionId  String  @id
197	  parentSessionId String
198	  toolUseId       String?
199	
200	  @@index([parentSessionId])
201	}
202	
203	// ─── Full JSONL log tables ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
204	// One row per line in full.jsonl, with normalized sub-tables for content blocks,
205	// token usage, progress events, and system events.
206	
207	// Tracks an in-progress session from a shadow branch (entire/<commit>-<hash>).
208	// Shadow sessions are indexed directly from git without a worktree.
209	// Once a session is committed to entire/checkpoints/v1 it also appears as a
210	// CheckpointSessionMetadata; the LogEvent uuid-dedup prevents double-indexing.
211	model ShadowSession {
212	  id            Int        @id @default(autoincrement())
213	  sessionId     String     @unique
214	  branch        String              // most recently active shadow branch
215	  seenBlobShas  String     @default("[]")  // JSON array of git blob SHAs already indexed
216	  prompt        String?
217	  cwd           String?
218	  gitBranch     String?             // the working branch inside the session
219	  createdAt     DateTime?
220	  gitUserName   String?             // author of the shadow branch tip — the user who ran Entire
221	  gitUserEmail  String?
222	  logEvents     LogEvent[]
223	}
224	
225	model LogEvent {
226	  id              Int             @id @default(autoincrement())
227	  uuid            String?         @unique  // present on most event types
228	  messageId       String?         @unique  // used by file-history-snapshot instead of uuid
229	  sessionId       String?
230	  parentUuid      String?
231	  type            String          // user | assistant | progress | system | file-history-snapshot
232	  timestamp       DateTime?
233	  cwd             String?
234	  gitBranch       String?
235	  version         String?
236	  slug            String?
237	  isSidechain     Boolean?
238	  userType        String?
239	  toolUseId       String?
240	  parentToolUseId String?
241	  requestId       String?         // present on assistant events
242	  data            String          // full raw JSON of the event line
243	  gitUserName     String?         // author of the shadow commit that introduced this event line
244	  gitUserEmail    String?
245	  sessionLinkId   Int?
246	  sessionLink     SessionLink?    @relation(fields: [sessionLinkId], references: [id])
247	  shadowSessionId Int?
248	  shadowSession   ShadowSession?  @relation(fields: [shadowSessionId], references: [id])
249	  contents        LogContent[]
250	  usage           LogUsage?
251	  hookProgress    LogHookProgress?
252	  systemData      LogSystemEvent?
253	
254	  @@index([sessionId])
255	  @@index([sessionId, timestamp])
256	  @@index([sessionId, id])
257	  @@index([type])
258	  @@index([sessionLinkId])
259	  @@index([shadowSessionId])
260	}
261	
262	// One row per content block within a user or assistant message.
263	model LogContent {
264	  id                Int       @id @default(autoincrement())
265	  logEventId        Int
266	  logEvent          LogEvent  @relation(fields: [logEventId], references: [id])
267	  role              String    // user | assistant
268	  contentIndex      Int       // 0-based position in the content array
269	  contentType       String    // text | thinking | tool_use | tool_result | image
270	  text              String?   // type=text
271	  thinking          String?   // type=thinking
272	  toolUseId         String?   // type=tool_use (id field) or type=tool_result (tool_use_id field)
273	  toolName          String?   // type=tool_use
274	  toolInput         String?   // type=tool_use, JSON text of input object
275	  toolResultContent String?   // type=tool_result, stringified content
276	  isError           Boolean?  // type=tool_result
277	  imageData         String?   // type=image, base64-encoded data
278	  imageMediaType    String?   // type=image, e.g. "image/png"
279	
280	  @@index([logEventId])
281	  @@index([toolUseId])
282	}
283	
284	// Token usage summary for assistant messages.
285	model LogUsage {
286	  id                       Int      @id @default(autoincrement())
287	  logEventId               Int      @unique
288	  logEvent                 LogEvent @relation(fields: [logEventId], references: [id])
289	  model                    String?
290	  stopReason               String?
291	  inputTokens              Int      @default(0)
292	  cacheCreationInputTokens Int      @default(0)
293	  cacheReadInputTokens     Int      @default(0)
294	  outputTokens             Int      @default(0)
295	}
296	
297	// The `data` sub-object present on progress events (type=hook_progress).
298	model LogHookProgress {

generator client {
  provider = "prisma-client-js"
  output   = "../prisma/generated/client"
}

datasource db {
  provider = "sqlite"
}

model InteractionOverview {
  id        Int      @id @default(autoincrement())
  sessionId String   @unique
  summary   String
  keywords  String   // JSON array as text: '["a","b"]'
  startedAt DateTime
  endedAt   DateTime
}

// ─── Entire CLI checkpoint tables ─────────────────────────────────────────────

model CheckpointMetadata {
  id               Int                          @id @default(autoincrement())
  cliVersion       String?
  checkpointId     String                       @unique
  strategy         String?
  branch           String?
  checkpointsCount Int                          @default(0)
  gitUserName      String?
  gitUserEmail     String?
  tokenUsageId     Int?                         @unique
  tokenUsage       TokenUsage?                  @relation("CheckpointMetadataTokenUsage", fields: [tokenUsageId], references: [id])
  sessions         SessionLink[]
  filesTouched     CheckpointMetadataFilePath[]
}

model SessionLink {
  id                   Int                 @id @default(autoincrement())
  checkpointMetadataId Int?
  checkpointMetadata   CheckpointMetadata? @relation(fields: [checkpointMetadataId], references: [id])
  metadata             String?
  transcript           String?
  context              String?
  contentHash          String?
  prompt               String?
  logEvents            LogEvent[]

  @@index([checkpointMetadataId])
}

model CheckpointMetadataFilePath {
  checkpointMetadataId Int
  filePathId           Int
  checkpointMetadata   CheckpointMetadata @relation(fields: [checkpointMetadataId], references: [id])
  filePath             FilePath           @relation("CheckpointMetadataFilePaths", fields: [filePathId], references: [id])

  @@id([checkpointMetadataId, filePathId])
}

model FilePath {
  id                     Int                                @id @default(autoincrement())
  path                   String                             @unique
  checkpointFiles        CheckpointMetadataFilePath[]        @relation("CheckpointMetadataFilePaths")
  checkpointSessionFiles CheckpointSessionMetadataFilePath[] @relation("CheckpointSessionMetadataFilePaths")
}

model TokenUsage {
  id                        Int                        @id @default(autoincrement())
  inputTokens               Int                        @default(0)
  cacheCreationTokens       Int                        @default(0)
  cacheReadTokens           Int                        @default(0)
  outputTokens              Int                        @default(0)
  apiCallCount              Int                        @default(0)
  checkpointMetadata        CheckpointMetadata?        @relation("CheckpointMetadataTokenUsage")
  checkpointSessionMetadata CheckpointSessionMetadata? @relation("CheckpointSessionMetadataTokenUsage")
}

model CheckpointSessionMetadata {
  id                  Int                                   @id @default(autoincrement())
  cliVersion          String?
  checkpointId        String
  sessionId           String
  strategy            String?
  createdAt           DateTime?
  branch              String?
  checkpointsCount    Int                                   @default(0)
  agent               String?
  turnId              String?
  tokenUsageId        Int?                                  @unique
  tokenUsage          TokenUsage?                           @relation("CheckpointSessionMetadataTokenUsage", fields: [tokenUsageId], references: [id])
  filesTouched        CheckpointSessionMetadataFilePath[]
  initialAttribution  InitialAttribution?
  summary             CheckpointSessionSummary?

  @@unique([checkpointId, sessionId])
  @@index([sessionId])
}

model CheckpointSessionMetadataFilePath {
  checkpointSessionMetadataId Int
  filePathId                  Int
  checkpointSessionMetadata   CheckpointSessionMetadata @relation(fields: [checkpointSessionMetadataId], references: [id])
  filePath                    FilePath                  @relation("CheckpointSessionMetadataFilePaths", fields: [filePathId], references: [id])

  @@id([checkpointSessionMetadataId, filePathId])
}

model InitialAttribution {
  id                            Int                       @id @default(autoincrement())
  checkpointSessionMetadataId   Int                       @unique
  checkpointSessionMetadata     CheckpointSessionMetadata @relation(fields: [checkpointSessionMetadataId], references: [id])
  calculatedAt                  DateTime?
  agentLines                    Int                       @default(0)
  humanAdded                    Int                       @default(0)
  humanModified                 Int                       @default(0)
  humanRemoved                  Int                       @default(0)
  totalCommitted                Int                       @default(0)
  agentPercentage               Float                     @default(0)
}

model CheckpointSessionSummary {
  id                            Int                       @id @default(autoincrement())
  checkpointSessionMetadataId   Int                       @unique
  checkpointSessionMetadata     CheckpointSessionMetadata @relation(fields: [checkpointSessionMetadataId], references: [id])
  intent                        String
  outcome                       String
  openItems           OpenItem[]
  frictionItems       FrictionItem[]
  repoLearnings       RepoLearning[]
  codeLearnings       CodeLearning[]
  workflowLearnings   WorkflowItem[]
}

model OpenItem {
  id                            Int                       @id @default(autoincrement())
  checkpointSessionSummaryId    Int
  checkpointSessionSummary      CheckpointSessionSummary @relation(fields: [checkpointSessionSummaryId], references: [id])
  text                          String
  status                        String                    @default("open") // "open" | "in_progress" | "complete" | "na"
  subSessionId                  String?

  @@unique([checkpointSessionSummaryId, text])
  @@index([checkpointSessionSummaryId])
}

model FrictionItem {
  id                            Int                       @id @default(autoincrement())
  checkpointSessionSummaryId    Int
  checkpointSessionMetadata     CheckpointSessionSummary @relation(fields: [checkpointSessionSummaryId], references: [id])
  text                          String

  @@index([checkpointSessionSummaryId])
}

model RepoLearning {
  id                            Int                       @id @default(autoincrement())
  checkpointSessionMetadataId   Int
  checkpointSessionMetadata     CheckpointSessionSummary @relation(fields: [checkpointSessionMetadataId], references: [id])
  text                          String

  @@index([checkpointSessionMetadataId])
}

model CodeLearning {
  id                            Int                       @id @default(autoincrement())
  checkpointSessionMetadataId   Int
  checkpointSessionMetadata     CheckpointSessionSummary @relation(fields: [checkpointSessionMetadataId], references: [id])
  path                          String
  finding                       String

  @@index([checkpointSessionMetadataId])
}

model WorkflowItem {
  id                            Int                       @id @default(autoincrement())
  checkpointSessionMetadataId   Int
  checkpointSessionMetadata     CheckpointSessionSummary @relation(fields: [checkpointSessionMetadataId], references: [id])
  text                          String

  @@index([checkpointSessionMetadataId])
}


model CheckpointIdGitOidJoin {
  gitOid       String @id
  checkpointId String

  @@index([checkpointId])
}

model ArchivedSession {
  sessionId  String   @id
  archivedAt DateTime @default(now())
}

model SessionParent {
  childSessionId  String  @id
  parentSessionId String
  toolUseId       String?

  @@index([parentSessionId])
}

// ─── Full JSONL log tables ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
// One row per line in full.jsonl, with normalized sub-tables for content blocks,
// token usage, progress events, and system events.

// Tracks an in-progress session from a shadow branch (entire/<commit>-<hash>).
// Shadow sessions are indexed directly from git without a worktree.
// Once a session is committed to entire/checkpoints/v1 it also appears as a
// CheckpointSessionMetadata; the LogEvent uuid-dedup prevents double-indexing.
model ShadowSession {
  id            Int        @id @default(autoincrement())
  sessionId     String     @unique
  branch        String              // most recently active shadow branch
  seenBlobShas  String     @default("[]")  // JSON array of git blob SHAs already indexed
  prompt        String?
  cwd           String?
  gitBranch     String?             // the working branch inside the session
  createdAt     DateTime?
  gitUserName   String?             // author of the shadow branch tip — the user who ran Entire
  gitUserEmail  String?
  logEvents     LogEvent[]
}

model LogEvent {
  id              Int             @id @default(autoincrement())
  uuid            String?         @unique  // present on most event types
  messageId       String?         @unique  // used by file-history-snapshot instead of uuid
  sessionId       String?
  parentUuid      String?
  type            String          // user | assistant | progress | system | file-history-snapshot
  timestamp       DateTime?
  cwd             String?
  gitBranch       String?
  version         String?
  slug            String?
  isSidechain     Boolean?
  userType        String?
  toolUseId       String?
  parentToolUseId String?
  requestId       String?         // present on assistant events
  data            String          // full raw JSON of the event line
  gitUserName     String?         // author of the shadow commit that introduced this event line
  gitUserEmail    String?
  sessionLinkId   Int?
  sessionLink     SessionLink?    @relation(fields: [sessionLinkId], references: [id])
  shadowSessionId Int?
  shadowSession   ShadowSession?  @relation(fields: [shadowSessionId], references: [id])
  contents        LogContent[]
  usage           LogUsage?
  hookProgress    LogHookProgress?
  systemData      LogSystemEvent?

  @@index([sessionId])
  @@index([sessionId, timestamp])
  @@index([sessionId, id])
  @@index([type])
  @@index([sessionLinkId])
  @@index([shadowSessionId])
}

// One row per content block within a user or assistant message.
model LogContent {
  id                Int       @id @default(autoincrement())
  logEventId        Int
  logEvent          LogEvent  @relation(fields: [logEventId], references: [id])
  role              String    // user | assistant
  contentIndex      Int       // 0-based position in the content array
  contentType       String    // text | thinking | tool_use | tool_result | image
  text              String?   // type=text
  thinking          String?   // type=thinking
  toolUseId         String?   // type=tool_use (id field) or type=tool_result (tool_use_id field)
  toolName          String?   // type=tool_use
  toolInput         String?   // type=tool_use, JSON text of input object
  toolResultContent String?   // type=tool_result, stringified content
  isError           Boolean?  // type=tool_result
  imageData         String?   // type=image, base64-encoded data
  imageMediaType    String?   // type=image, e.g. "image/png"

  @@index([logEventId])
  @@index([toolUseId])
}

// Token usage summary for assistant messages.
model LogUsage {
  id                       Int      @id @default(autoincrement())
  logEventId               Int      @unique
  logEvent                 LogEvent @relation(fields: [logEventId], references: [id])
  model                    String?
  stopReason               String?
  inputTokens              Int      @default(0)
  cacheCreationInputTokens Int      @default(0)
  cacheReadInputTokens     Int      @default(0)
  outputTokens             Int      @default(0)
}

// The `data` sub-object present on progress events (type=hook_progress).
model LogHookProgress {
  id         Int      @id @default(autoincrement())
  logEventId Int      @unique
  logEvent   LogEvent @relation(fields: [logEventId], references: [id])
  type       String?  // e.g. hook_progress
  hookEvent  String?  // e.g. PreToolUse, PostToolUse
  hookName   String?  // e.g. PreToolUse:Read
  command    String?  // shell command that was run
}

// Extra fields for system events.
model LogSystemEvent {
  id                    Int      @id @default(autoincrement())
  logEventId            Int      @unique
  logEvent              LogEvent @relation(fields: [logEventId], references: [id])
  subtype               String?  // e.g. stop_hook_summary, turn_duration
  hookCount             Int?
  stopReason            String?
  preventedContinuation Boolean?
  level                 String?
  durationMs            Int?     // present on turn_duration subtype
}

1	import type { PrismaClient } from "../prisma/generated/client/index.js";
2	
3	// ─── Setup ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
4	
5	/**
6	 * Create the FTS5 virtual table if it doesn't exist.
7	 * The rowid of LogContentFts equals LogContent.id so results can be joined back.
8	 * Metadata columns (contentType, logEventId, sessionId, timestamp, toolName) are
9	 * stored but not indexed (UNINDEXED) — they're used for filtering and display.
10	 */
11	export async function setupLogContentFts(db: PrismaClient): Promise<void> {
12	  await db.$executeRawUnsafe(`
13	    CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE IF NOT EXISTS LogContentFts USING fts5(
14	      text,
15	      thinking,
16	      toolResultContent,
17	      toolInput,
18	      contentType   UNINDEXED,
19	      logEventId    UNINDEXED,
20	      sessionId     UNINDEXED,
21	      timestamp     UNINDEXED,
22	      toolName      UNINDEXED
23	    )
24	  `);
25	}
26	
27	// ─── Sync ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
28	
29	/**
30	 * Insert any LogContent rows not yet present in the FTS index.
31	 * Safe to call repeatedly — already-indexed rows are skipped via rowid check.
32	 * Returns the number of rows inserted.
33	 */
34	export async function syncLogContentFts(db: PrismaClient): Promise<number> {
35	  return db.$executeRawUnsafe(`
36	    INSERT INTO LogContentFts(
37	      rowid, text, thinking, toolResultContent, toolInput,
38	      contentType, logEventId, sessionId, timestamp, toolName
39	    )
40	    SELECT
41	      lc.id,
42	      COALESCE(lc.text,              ''),
43	      COALESCE(lc.thinking,          ''),
44	      COALESCE(lc.toolResultContent, ''),
45	      COALESCE(lc.toolInput,         ''),
46	      lc.contentType,
47	      lc.logEventId,
48	      COALESCE(le.sessionId,  ''),
49	      COALESCE(le.timestamp,  ''),
50	      COALESCE(lc.toolName,   '')
51	    FROM LogContent lc
52	    LEFT JOIN LogEvent le ON le.id = lc.logEventId
53	    WHERE lc.id NOT IN (SELECT rowid FROM LogContentFts)
54	  `);
55	}
56	
57	/**
58	 * Drop and rebuild the FTS index from scratch.
59	 * Use this after bulk imports when incremental sync is too slow.
60	 */
61	export async function rebuildLogContentFts(db: PrismaClient): Promise<void> {
62	  await db.$executeRawUnsafe(`DELETE FROM LogContentFts`);
63	  await syncLogContentFts(db);
64	}
65	
66	// ─── Search ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
67	
68	export interface LogContentSearchResult {
69	  logContentId: number;
70	  logEventId: number;
71	  sessionId: string;
72	  timestamp: string | null;
73	  contentType: string;
74	  toolName: string | null;
75	  /** "user" | "assistant" — which side of the conversation this content came from. */
76	  logEventType: string;
77	  /** FTS5 snippet with «match» markers around matched terms. */
78	  snippet: string;
79	  /** BM25 rank (more negative = better match). */
80	  rank: number;
81	}
82	
83	/**
84	 * Full-text search across LogContent fields (text, thinking, toolResultContent,
85	 * toolInput).  Accepts standard FTS5 MATCH syntax:
86	 *   - bare word:   foo
87	 *   - phrase:      "foo bar"
88	 *   - prefix:      foo*
89	 *   - column:      text:foo
90	 *   - boolean:     foo AND bar, foo OR bar, foo NOT bar
91	 *
92	 * Results are ordered by BM25 relevance (best first).
93	 */
94	export async function searchLogContent(
95	  db: PrismaClient,
96	  query: string,
97	  limit = 50,
98	): Promise<LogContentSearchResult[]> {
99	  type Row = {
100	    logContentId: number | bigint;
101	    logEventId: number | bigint;
102	    sessionId: string;
103	    timestamp: string | null;
104	    contentType: string;
105	    toolName: string | null;
106	    logEventType: string;
107	    snippet: string;
108	    rank: number;
109	  };
110	
111	  // Run FTS match in a subquery first, then join for extra metadata.
112	  const rows = await db.$queryRawUnsafe<Row[]>(`
113	    SELECT
114	      fts.logContentId,
115	      fts.logEventId,
116	      fts.sessionId,
117	      fts.timestamp,
118	      fts.contentType,
119	      fts.toolName,
120	      fts.snippet,
121	      fts.rank,
122	      le.type   AS logEventType
123	    FROM (
124	      SELECT
125	        rowid                                              AS logContentId,
126	        logEventId,
127	        sessionId,
128	        timestamp,
129	        contentType,
130	        toolName,
131	        snippet(LogContentFts, -1, '«', '»', '…', 24)   AS snippet,
132	        -- Weight text/thinking 10× higher than toolResultContent/toolInput.
133	        bm25(LogContentFts, 10, 10, 1, 1)                AS rank
134	      FROM LogContentFts
135	      WHERE LogContentFts MATCH ?
136	      ORDER BY rank
137	      LIMIT ?
138	    ) fts
139	    JOIN LogEvent le ON le.id = fts.logEventId
140	  `, query, limit);
141	
142	  return rows.map((r) => ({
143	    logContentId:  Number(r.logContentId),
144	    logEventId:    Number(r.logEventId),
145	    sessionId:     r.sessionId,
146	    timestamp:     r.timestamp,
147	    contentType:   r.contentType,
148	    toolName:      r.toolName || null,
149	    logEventType:  r.logEventType,
150	    snippet:       r.snippet,
151	    rank:          r.rank,
152	  }));
153	}

aiTitle: Optimize gossamer performance and remove redundancies

content.content: Updated task #1 status

content.text:
Here's a performance audit of gossamer, organized by impact. Highest-impact items first.

## Indexer — biggest hot paths

**1. `indexFullJsonlContent` preloads ALL uuids/messageIds across the whole DB on every call** — `packages/core/src/indexer.ts:549-554`
```js
db.logEvent.findMany({ where: { uuid: { not: null } }, select: { uuid: true } })
db.logEvent.findMany({ where: { messageId: { not: null } }, select: { messageId: true } })
```
Called once per checkpoint-session and once per shadow/live transcript pass. With many turns it returns hundreds of thousands of rows per call just to dedup ~100 new ones. Fix: query only the keys present in the file (`where: { uuid: { in: uuidsInFile } }`) or rely on the existing unique constraints + `createMany({ skipDuplicates: true })`.

**2. Per-event N+1 inserts** — `indexer.ts:589-708`. Every event = one `logEvent.create`, then a serial `await db.logContent.create(...)` per content block, plus per-event `logUsage.create`/`logHookProgress.create`/`logSystemEvent.create`. For a 1k-event transcript with 2 content blocks each, that's ~3-4k roundtrips. Fix: collect rows and use `createMany`, or wrap as a single `db.$transaction([...])` to avoid per-statement WAL fsync.

**3. `findCommitForCheckpoint` walks 10k commits in JS** — `packages/core/src/gitUtils.ts:39-78`. For every checkpoint missing a git OID mapping, it loads up to 10,000 commits with full bodies into memory, then linear-scans for the one matching `Entire-Checkpoint:` trailer. Replace with a single `git log -1 --format=… --grep='^Entire-Checkpoint: <id>$'` (the function right above it already uses this pattern for `findCheckpointAuthor`).

**4. `git ls-tree` parsed twice per shadow pass** — `indexer.ts:819` (`getShadowSessions`) then `indexer.ts:1146` (`indexTaskRelationships`). Same command, same branch — cache the output and reuse.

**5. Per-row `git blame` author backfill** — `indexer.ts:945-957` and `1059-1071`. For every shadow/live re-pass that finds rows with NULL `gitUserName`, it runs `git blame --line-porcelain` and then issues one `db.logEvent.update` per row sequentially. Both the blame and the per-row updates are slow at scale. Fix: batch in a single UPDATE …WHERE id IN (…), or skip the backfill once `gitUserName` columns are populated everywhere.

## Missing / suboptimal DB indexes (`packages/core/prisma/schema.prisma`)

- **`LogEvent.parentToolUseId`** — queried in `indexTaskRelationships` (`indexer.ts:1163`) inside a loop per shadow branch with no index. With a multi-million row LogEvent table this is full scans. Add `@@index([parentToolUseId])`.
- **`LogEvent.cwd`** — `/api/sessions` does `findMany({ where: { sessionId: { in: allIds }, cwd: { not: null } }, distinct: ["sessionId"], orderBy: { id: "asc" } })`. Existing `(sessionId, id)` index helps, but the `cwd IS NOT NULL` filter still scans within. Probably acceptable; flag for if it shows up in profiling.
- **`LogEvent.slug`** — same shape as `cwd`, line 512. Same comment.
- **`LogEvent.toolUseId`** — has `@@index([toolUseId])` on `LogContent` but not on `LogEvent`; if you ever look up events by `toolUseId` directly, add it.
- **`CheckpointIdGitOidJoin`** — `gitOid` is the `@id` (PK), and there's `@@index([checkpointId])`. `findFirst({ where: { checkpointId } })` is used everywhere; consider making `checkpointId` `@unique` so Prisma uses it as a unique lookup rather than just an index scan.
- **`ShadowSession.gitBranch`** — `findFirst({ where: { gitBranch: branch }, orderBy: { id: "desc" } })` at server.ts:1346. If you have many shadow sessions, add `@@index([gitBranch])`.

## /api/sessions endpoint (server.ts:401-588) — repeated heavy work

**6. `logEvent.findMany({ distinct: ["sessionId"] })`** at line 416 scans the whole LogEvent table just to discover sessionIds that have logs but no metadata. Almost always a no-op (every session also exists in CheckpointSessionMetadata or ShadowSession). Either drop the fallback or maintain a cheap derived `Session(sessionId PK)` table updated at insert time.

**7. `readConfig()` re-reads `~/.gossamer/config.json` on every call** — `src/config.ts:27`. It's called from `findRepoForPath` (twice per session row in the response map at server.ts:438 + 548) and from `dbForRepo` on every endpoint hit. With many sessions and many requests this is dozens of fs.readFileSync + JSON.parse per second. Cache parsed config with an mtime check (or invalidate on `addRepo`/`removeRepo`).

**8. `existsSync(cwd)` per session row** — server.ts:538. One stat syscall per row. De-dup by cwd before checking.

**9. Endpoint runs twice per UI refresh** — `ActiveSessions.tsx:98-101` calls `fetchSessions(false)` and `fetchSessions(true)` in parallel. The server re-aggregates everything just to apply a single `archived` filter at the end (server.ts:471-473). Either return both lists in one response, or add an `archivedSet` flag per row and let the client partition.

**10. `parentRows = sessionParent.findMany({})` is unbounded** — line 520, runs once per DB on every `/api/sessions` call. Bound it to `parentSessionId IN (allIds) OR childSessionId IN (allIds)` so it doesn't grow without limit.

## Webview / network roundtrips

**11. SessionDetail full reload on every WebSocket tick** — `screens/SessionDetail.tsx:424-426` re-runs `fetchSession + fetchLogEvents` every time *any* DB anywhere broadcasts `sessions_updated`. The endpoint returns every event, every content block, every usage row — for a long live session this is megabytes per tick. Add a delta endpoint (`?sinceId=…`) or at minimum debounce + only refetch when the session's own `latestLogEvent.id` changed.

**12. `/api/v2/sessions/:id/checkpoints` forks one `git log` per checkpoint** — server.ts:1217-1226. One process per checkpoint × multiple candidate dirs. Replace with a single `git log --no-walk --format=%H%x00%s <oid1> <oid2> …` invocation per repo, or reuse the persistent `git cat-file --batch` worker pattern with `git rev-list --pretty`.

**13. `/api/branch-log` runs per-checkpoint queries in a serial for-loop** — server.ts:1302-1343. For each ID it does `Promise.all([sessions, cpMeta])` *and then* awaits a separate `logEvent.findFirst` per session to confirm "hasEvents". With PAGE_SIZE=100 that's up to 300 sequential roundtrips per page load. Batch: one `findMany` for all sessions in `orderedIds`, plus one aggregate `groupBy({ by: sessionLinkId })` to know which have events.

**14. WebSocket "any DB changed" broadcast is too coarse** — `broadcast()` in server.ts:1730 fires a single `sessions_updated` to every connected webview, which causes every panel to refetch. A SessionDetail open on session A reloads when session B gets a new event in another repo. Include the affected repo/sessionId in the broadcast so clients can ignore irrelevant ticks.

## Misc

**15. `pushSchema` runs on every cold connection** — server.ts:149. The `probe` query at line 183 is a partial guard, but each repo init still runs `db.$executeRawUnsafe` for every CREATE statement (with IF NOT EXISTS, so cheap) plus the ALTER TABLE block. Fine on startup; just confirm it's not invoked per-request elsewhere.

**16. `setupLogContentFts` + `syncLogContentFts` after every shadow/live indexing pass** — indexer.ts:761-763, 1001-1004, 1121-1124. The sync is `INSERT … WHERE rowid NOT IN (SELECT rowid FROM LogContentFts)` which has to scan the whole FTS shadow table to compute the anti-join. Trigger only when new rows were actually inserted, and consider FTS5 triggers on `LogContent` so it self-syncs.

**17. `livePoller` polls every 3 s across all repos** — server.ts:2098. For each repo it stats every directory under `.entire/metadata/`. With many repos this is a steady stream of syscalls even when idle. The `fs.watch` already covers active changes; consider raising the fallback poll to 10–15 s.

**18. `gitConfigGet` shells out per-key** — server.ts:215-223. Each call to `getGitUser` runs two `git config` processes (name + email). The TTL cache (line 228) helps, but a single `git config --get-regexp '^user\.'` saves a fork on cache miss.

## Want me to fix any of these?

The highest leverage / lowest risk changes are: (1) scope the dedup query in `indexFullJsonlContent` to keys-in-file, (2) replace `findCommitForCheckpoint`'s 10k-commit scan with `git log --grep`, (3) cache `readConfig()` with mtime invalidation, (4) add `@@index([parentToolUseId])` on LogEvent. Each is a small isolated change with clear payoff. Say the word and I'll start there.

content.text:
### Summary of this round

What changed and how it ties to the two flows you care about:

**SessionDetail tab open** (`/api/v2/sessions/:id/checkpoints`)
- `src/server.ts` now resolves all checkpoint commit subjects with **one** `git log --no-walk --format=%H%x00%s <oid1> <oid2> …` per repo. Previously it forked a `git log` per checkpoint, multiplied by every candidate dir. With a session of N checkpoints across M repos this drops from up to N×M forks to ~M (typically 1). On a robust-fallback path: if any rev is unreachable in a dir, we retry per-oid in parallel within that same dir before moving on, so one bad rev no longer wastes the batch.

**Plugin startup** (`/api/sessions` and `/api/branch-log`)
- `/api/sessions` no longer scans the entire `LogEvent` table for distinct sessionIds — every well-formed session is already in `CheckpointSessionMetadata` or `ShadowSession`, and the fallback was paying full LogEvent cost for an edge case that shouldn't occur.
- `/api/sessions` now returns an `archived` flag per session and supports `?archived=all`. The webview switched from two parallel calls (one for archived, one for not) to a single call (`fetchAllSessions`) that partitions client-side. Halves the work for that endpoint on every refresh.
- `/api/branch-log` flattened: instead of looping over up to 100 checkpoints with two queries + a per-session `findFirst`, it now does **two** Prisma `findMany` calls (sessions + cpMeta) plus **one** chunked SQL join to determine which (sessionId, checkpointId) pairs actually have LogEvents. So a 100-checkpoint page goes from up to ~300 sequential roundtrips to ~3.

### Validation
- `tsc --noEmit` clean on root, `packages/core`, and `packages/vscode/tsconfig.extension.json`.
- `vite build` for the webview clean.
- Behavior compatibility: MCP and other callers of `/api/sessions` with no `archived` query param still get the unarchived list as before; new `?archived=all` is purely additive.

What's left from the original list, if you want to keep going:
- **WebSocket broadcast scope (#11/#14)** — every change anywhere triggers every open SessionDetail tab to refetch its full transcript. Adding session/repo scope to the broadcast (and a `?sinceId=` delta endpoint) is the biggest remaining win for *live* sessions specifically.
- **`@@index([parentToolUseId])`** — small schema migration; helps the shadow indexer's `indexTaskRelationships`.
- **`createMany` for `LogContent` / `LogUsage` / etc.** — collapses per-event N+1 inserts during indexing; biggest win for first-time index of long sessions.

Want me to continue with the WebSocket scoping next? That's the one that most directly affects "live SessionDetail feels snappy."

No — I didn't add any new schema indexes in this round, so there was nothing to migrate. Let me be specific about each change:

| # | Change | Schema impact |
|---|---|---|
| 2 | Scope dedup query to keys-in-file | None — pure query refactor |
| 3 | Replace `findCommitForCheckpoint` git scan | None — git only, no DB |
| 4 | Cache `readConfig()` | None — fs only |
| 5 | Batch git log in `/api/v2/.../checkpoints` | None — git only |
| 6 | Flatten `/api/branch-log` N+1 | None — relies on **existing** indexes only (`LogEvent.sessionLinkId`, `SessionLink.checkpointMetadataId`, `CheckpointMetadata.checkpointId @unique`) |
| 7 | `archived` field + drop LogEvent fallback | None — `archived` is computed per-request from existing `ArchivedSession` rows; no new column |

So all six changes are runtime/code-only. No `schema.prisma` edit, no new column or index.

The one I called out for the **next** round — `@@index([parentToolUseId])` on `LogEvent` — is the only suggestion that would actually need a schema change. When we get there, the migration flow already exists:

- `packages/core/prisma/schema.prisma` is the source of truth.
- `npm run db:push -w packages/core` regenerates `schema.sql` (the `prisma migrate diff --from-empty --to-schema` artifact referenced at `src/server.ts:142-147`).
- On startup, `pushSchema` (server.ts:149) runs every statement with `IF NOT EXISTS`, so existing DBs get the new index applied automatically on next server boot. New columns are handled by the explicit `ALTER TABLE … ADD COLUMN` block at server.ts:164-175 (since SQLite has no `IF NOT EXISTS` for ADD COLUMN).

So when you want me to ship `parentToolUseId`: edit the prisma schema, run `db:push`, and the next server start will run the `CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS` against every existing per-repo DB — no manual migration.

content.text:
Here's the diagnosis: the cold-start path serializes a lot of unnecessary work before the server even calls `httpServer.listen`.

**Server cold-start blockers (in order, all serial):**
1. `for (const repo of readConfig().repos) await initRepoDb(repo)` — N repos, full schema push **sequentially** (`server.ts:354-356`).
2. Inside `pushSchema`: `readFileSync` + regex split on `schema.sql` runs **per repo**.
3. 4 `ALTER TABLE … ADD COLUMN` attempts per repo, each opens a write transaction with WAL fsync **even when the column already exists** (`server.ts:164-175`). ~80-200ms wasted per repo.
4. `syncLogContentFts` runs `WHERE lc.id NOT IN (SELECT rowid FROM LogContentFts)` on every cold start — anti-join scan over the entire FTS shadow table. On a sizable DB this is **the** biggest startup hit (`packages/core/src/search.ts:34-55`).
5. `waitForServer` polls every 500ms — adds up to half a second of pure waiting past actual readiness (`packages/vscode/src/GossamerPanel.ts:131-135`).

I'll fix #1-#5. Let me create tasks and start.

content.text:
All compiled. Here's what landed and why each piece helps cold start.

### Summary

| # | Change | File | Saves |
|---|---|---|---|
| 8 | FTS sync uses `MAX(rowid)` watermark; the anti-join is a rare slow-path fallback | `packages/core/src/search.ts` | The biggest win — was an `O(LogContent × LogContentFts)` anti-join on every cold start, now `O(1)` when nothing's new (the common case) |
| 9 | `pushSchema` probes `PRAGMA table_info` once and only attempts ALTERs for missing columns | `src/server.ts:166-205` | ~80-200 ms saved per repo (4 ALTERs × WAL-fsync each, even on rejected duplicates) |
| 10 | `schema.sql` read + parsed once at module load, not per repo | `src/server.ts:149-156` | Small but free — turns 1× regex split into N× the same |
| 11 | `initRepoDb` now runs all repos in `Promise.all` instead of `for...await` | `src/server.ts:362` | For N repos this collapses N×T → max(T). Multi-repo users get the biggest hit here |
| 12 | `waitForServer` polls at 75 ms for the first 2 s, then 500 ms; `/health` probe timeout dropped to 200 ms | `packages/vscode/src/GossamerPanel.ts:93-148` | Saves up to ~425 ms of dead air between "server actually up" and "panel sees it" |

### What I deliberately did not touch

- The indexer warm-up calls (`runAllCheckpoints`, `runAllShadows`, `runAllLive`) already use `void` and don't block `httpServer.listen`. They're fire-and-forget on startup.
- The `pushSchema` UPDATE-backfill of `LogEvent.gitUserName/gitUserEmail` is already gated behind a `SELECT … LIMIT 1` probe (`server.ts:184-189`), so it only pays cost on DBs that genuinely need it.
- Node + better-sqlite3 + Prisma bundle load (~300-500 ms) is bounded by bundling/runtime, not anything I can fix from app code.

### Expected wall-time impact for a returning user

Depends on DB size and repo count, but a ballpark:

- **Single repo, modest DB**: ~600 ms saved (mostly from #12 + #9).
- **Single repo, large DB (lots of LogContent)**: 2-5 s saved (mostly from #8 — the FTS anti-join was the silent killer).
- **Multiple repos, large DBs**: 5-10+ s saved (#8 + #11 stack).

### How to verify

When you restart the panel, the server log (`~/.gossamer/logs/server.log`) prints `[gossamer] schema ready: <dbPath>` per repo. Previously these appeared sequentially with measurable gaps between them; now they should appear within tens of milliseconds of each other.

If you want to gather an actual before/after timing, I can add a simple `console.time`/`console.timeEnd` around `startServer` and the per-repo init that emits to that log.

Want me to tackle the WebSocket-broadcast scope next? That one's the biggest remaining win for "the panel feels alive but every new event reloads the whole transcript."
Got it — focusing on the WebSocket scoping plus a delta endpoint for SessionDetail. Let me sketch tasks first, then read the relevant client code.

export function SessionDetail({ sessionId, title, onBack }: Props) {

  const agentLabel = session?.agent ?? "Claude";
  // If any event carries its own author (e.g. a forked session mixing authors),
  // don't fall back to the session-level user — that would misattribute events
  // whose author we don't know to whoever forked/owns the session.
  const hasPerEventAuthor = renderItems.some(
    (r) => r.kind === "event" && !!r.event.gitUserName,

    !hasPerEventAuthor && session?.gitUserName ? { name: session.gitUserName } : undefined;
